objective: Convert the real Web Audio signal already observed during VieNeu WAV playback into bounded audio-spectral viseme cues, publish those renderer-safe cues to the avatar service at a maximum 10 Hz, and make both SVG and VRM presentations consume the same viseme/intensity state. The pipeline must remain explicitly heuristic and non-phoneme-accurate.
non_goals: Do not infer visemes from raw TTS text, canned timelines, random animation or synthetic demo values in the product path.
Do not label the spectral heuristic as phoneme alignment, forced alignment or speech recognition.
Do not store raw audio, frequency bins, text or generated viseme timelines.
Do not change VieNeu model weights, provider inference or base model behavior.
Do not expose analyser data outside renderer-safe viseme/intensity cues.
Do not run deep latency/soak gates or mark M07 complete.
contracts: Classifier input is only current Web Audio time/frequency byte arrays plus sample rate and FFT size; product output is one of sil/A/I/U/E/O, intensity 0..1 and accuracy audio_spectral_heuristic.
Silence threshold closes the mouth immediately; non-silence classification is stabilized before publication to reduce jitter.
Backend publication uses source speech.output, mode tts-playback and current correlation/session/utterance identifiers, at no more than 10 requests per second with only one request in flight.
Playback start no longer sends a fixed fake A viseme; playback finish sends sil with zero intensity.
Avatar service remains the validator/source of truth and continues to report phonemeAccurate false.
Desktop VRM maps only allowlisted avatar visemes to standard VRM vowel expression names and uses backend intensity; sil or unknown viseme closes all vowel expressions.
Raw audio arrays and frequency bins are not logged, persisted or sent to the backend.
Existing code-native SVG and VRM fallbacks remain functional.
allowed_dependencies: Browser Web Audio AnalyserNode already used by M07-S2.
Existing avatar loopback cue API and @pixiv/three-vrm expression manager.
No new package, model, weight, voice or avatar asset.
acceptance_criteria: Pure Node unit tests cover silence, each output viseme class, bounded intensity, invalid input and stabilizer behavior.
Avatar unit tests continue to reject unknown visemes and report phonemeAccurate false with audio-spectral mode.
Dev Console syntax/startup tests pass and the classifier script loads before app.js.
Loopback static transport serves only the explicitly allowlisted audio-viseme.js path with JavaScript content type.
A real VieNeu WAV browser workflow observes at least one non-sil audio-derived viseme, intensity greater than zero and speaking state while audio is playing, then sil/idle after playback.
Desktop typecheck/tests/smoke pass and VRM expression mapping contains no speaking-state fake mouth weight.
Asset manifest hashes match the changed renderer sources.
No raw analyser arrays, audio or input text are added to logs or API payloads.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
